content.claim: Hungary's per-pupil spending is comparatively low internationally at approximately $7,115 USD equivalent (rank 25 of 29 OECD/partner countries, 2022), constraining fiscal slack available to subsidize higher per-pupil overhead costs of small rural schools.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Comparison to OECD average does not establish optimal spending level
Low baseline spending may reflect policy choice rather than constraint
Does not directly measure marginal cost of rural school operation vs. consolidation
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
